summary: Spring ’90 high‑energy show featuring epic Blow Away solo & perfect China Cat → Rider
blurb: The Knick run delivers a blazing first set with Hell In A Bucket, Dupree’s Diamond Blues and a fire‑filled Blow Away. The second set shines with the classic China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider segue and a soaring Morning Dew into Brokedown Palace encore. Brent Mydland’s keyboards are magical throughout.
review:
On March 26, 1990 at Albany’s Knickerbocker Arena the Dead turned in one of the spring‑90 era’s most electrifying performances. The first set roared to life with a thunderous Hell In A Bucket, a ripping Dupree’s Diamond Blues and a jaw‑dropping Blow Away solo that several fans called “the best ever.” Brent Mydland was in top form, laying down tight, soaring keyboards on Blow Away and weaving his signature 1990 MIDI solo into the mix. 

The second set kept the momentum rolling: Victim Or The Crime segued flawlessly into a blistering China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider that crackled with Jerry’s crystal‑clear tone, followed by a powerful Man Smart, Woman Smarter and a tight Drums/Space stretch. A triumphant I Need A Miracle led straight into Dear Mr. Fantasy, Gimme Some Lovin’ and an uplifting Morning Dew that built into a heartfelt Brokedown Palace encore. Across audience FM broadcasts, matrix feeds and several soundboard sources the show is praised for its high‑octane energy, immaculate set flow, and especially Brent’s magical keyboards and Jerry’s soaring lead work.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: All twelve analyzed recordings are marked positive, with repeated praise for the band’s high‑energy performance, standout solos (Blow Away, China Cat > Rider), and Brent Mydland’s keyboard brilliance. No negative sentiment appears, just consistent accolades across sources.

best_recording.reason: Crisp FM broadcast with a clear, balanced mix and no audio flaws; captures the full show’s energy in excellent quality.

key_highlights: Brent Mydland delivers magical keyboards throughout, especially on Blow Away and China Cat Sunflower.
Jerry Garcia’s solo on Blow Away is hailed as “best ever” with a crystal‑clear tone.
The classic China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider segue crackles with energy and flawless execution.
A soaring Morning Dew into an emotional Brokedown Palace encore caps the night.

band_performance.Jerry: Garcia’s tone shines on every song, delivering a blistering Blow Away solo and flawless lead work through China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider and the closing Brokedown Palace.
band_performance.Phil: Lesh provides a huge, powerful bass presence, especially noticeable during Victim/China Cat transition; his foundation underpins the high‑energy set.
band_performance.Bob: Weir adds solid rhythm guitar and occasional vocal harmonies, complementing the tight band dynamic.
band_performance.Brent: Mydland is at his zenith, offering magical keyboard textures, tight Blow Away comping, a standout MIDI solo, and driving the flow of the second‑set jams.
band_performance.Mickey: Hart locks in with crisp, purposeful fills that drive the rhythmic momentum throughout both sets.
band_performance.Billy: Kreutzmann matches Hart’s precision, providing a solid dual‑drum foundation that keeps the groove tight.
